title: hydrocharis morsus-ranae subsp. vivipara
intro: Hydrocharis morsus-ranae subsp. vivipara, also known as the 'living Burdock', is an aquatic plant native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its distinctive leaves that can grow up to 30 cm in length and its unique ability to grow from rhizomes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hydrocharis morsus-ranae subsp. vivipara include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting skin health and wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves and rhizomes are boiled in water to extract its medicinal properties.
